CR_NLText: US Inflation Reduction Act Section 30D requires that for an EV to qualify for the $7,500 clean-vehicle tax credit, all critical battery minerals (lithium, cobalt, nickel, manganese, graphite) must be extracted or processed in the United States or in a country with a US Free Trade Agreement (FTA), OR recycled in North America. As of effective date 2024-01-01, the FTA-eligible countries are: Australia, Bahrain, Canada, Chile, Colombia, Costa Rica, Dominican Republic, El Salvador, Guatemala, Honduras, Israel, Jordan, South Korea, Mexico, Morocco, Nicaragua, Oman, Panama, Peru, Singapore. Vehicles registered in the USA whose BatteryPassport BPass_OriginCty is NOT in this list (for example Democratic Republic of Congo, Indonesia, China, Russia) FAIL Section 30D mineral compliance and lose the $7,500 customer credit. The agent must apply this rule whenever asked about IRA, US EV credit, or mineral-origin compliance.

EU Battery Regulation 2023/1542 mandates a digital battery passport for every EV battery placed on the EU market from 18 February 2027. The passport must include: state-of-health (BPass_SoH%), cradle-to-gate carbon footprint (BPass_kgCO2e), mineral country of origin (BPass_OriginCty), and recycling content disclosure. ConnectedVehicles whose CV_RegMarket is in the EU and which lack a HAS_BATT_PASS link to a complete BatteryPassport FAIL EU compliance. The agent must apply this rule when asked about EU battery compliance, market access, or passport completeness.

NHTSA Title 49 requires manufacturers to notify NHTSA of any safety-related defect or non-compliance within 5 business days of discovery. For RecallCampaigns where the linked QualityEvent has QE_Severity = Critical, owner notification (via dealer or direct) must begin within 60 days of NHTSA notification. Failure to comply carries civil penalties up to $135M per violation. The agent must check Recall_OpenDt for any open Critical recall and flag any campaign approaching the 60-day owner-notification deadline.

NHTSA expects recall completion rate to exceed 85% within 18 months of recall opening. Below-threshold completion may trigger expanded recall scope, civil penalty action, or mandatory dealer-routing supervision. The agent must, when asked about recall progress, compare the count of ConnectedVehicles linked via AFFECTS_VEHICLE that have a corresponding completed RepairTicket FOLLOWS_TSB with the recall's REQUIRES_TSB target, against the total recall scope, and warn if projected to miss 85% by 18 months from Recall_OpenDt.

EU Corporate Sustainability Reporting Directive (CSRD) requires Scope 3 Category 11 (use-of-product) emissions disclosure for large EU companies from FY2024. For automotive OEMs, Scope 3 Cat 11 represents approximately 80-95% of total lifecycle emissions and must be calculated per vehicle using the registration-market electricity grid factor and the BatteryPassport BPass_kgCO2e cradle-to-gate value. Vehicles registered in EU markets without sufficient BatteryPassport CO2e data cannot be reported and create CSRD audit risk. The agent must apply this rule when asked about CSRD readiness, ESG audit risk, or carbon disclosure completeness.

NovaDrive Internal: WarrantyPolicy WPOL-EVPK-8Y and WPOL-HV-8Y cover EV battery pack and high-voltage components for 8 years (96 months) or 160,000 km, whichever comes first. BPass_SoH below 70 percent within the warranty window triggers automatic pack-replacement eligibility regardless of cause. The agent must apply this rule when asked whether a specific VIN is eligible for free pack replacement, by checking VehicleOwner Owner_StartDt + 8 years against today, CV_OdoKm against 160000, and BPass_SoH against 70%.

NovaDrive Internal Goodwill Policy: For vehicles within 5 percent of the warranty mileage boundary OR within 60 days of warranty expiration, cover 50 percent of labor and 100 percent of TSB-mandated parts when the failure mode matches an active TSB. Goodwill claims should be filed with WarrantyClaim WClaim_State = 'Goodwill', not 'Approved'. The agent must recommend goodwill treatment whenever a customer is just outside warranty AND the failure matches a TSB linked via FOLLOWS_TSB.

NovaDrive Security Policy: Any SoftwareRelease with SwRel_Severity = 'Critical' and a non-empty SwRel_CVE must be patched on every affected ConnectedVehicle within 30 calendar days of SwRel_PublishDt. ConnectedVehicles whose most recent OTAUpdate for that software stack has OTA_Status in ('Failed','Pending') after 14 days must be flagged for dealer-assisted manual update. The agent must apply this SLA when asked about software-vulnerability exposure, patch readiness, or OTA campaign status.
